steps.title: Compare averages and extremes
steps.content: The meta-analysis found observable differences in every study. On average, fish studies showed larger differences than bird studies. But for every class of animals examined, some individual studies showed differences well above the average for fish.

steps.title: Draw the supported conclusion
steps.content: Because birds are one class of animals examined, some bird studies had differences well above the fish average. That means some bird studies found larger effects than at least some fish studies, even though fish studies were larger on average.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A concerns whether effects were detrimental, which is not discussed. Choice B says effects are likely more pronounced for birds overall, contradicting the fish average. Choice C speculates about one common carp study versus an average not given for common carp. The supported comparison is between some bird studies and some fish studies. The answer is D.
